videos.title: AI Didn’t Kill ETL — It Made Data Modeling More Important
videos.description:
Is ETL dead in the AI era? Not even close.

In this Guy in a Cube video, Patrick LeBlanc responds to a great community discussion around the AI-native data stack, ETL, data modeling, semantic models, medallion architecture, and what AI agents really need from your data.

This conversation was sparked by a Reddit thread in the Microsoft Fabric community:
https://www.reddit.com/r/MicrosoftFabric/s/ejV0HQeTd2

Patrick is going to show you why AI does not remove the need for ETL, data warehouses, or

videos.title: Why Only AI Training Can Save the Economy
videos.description: Large-scale AI training offers the only practical path to align AI labs' token-driven revenue growth with enterprise spending constraints. A shift from seat-based subscriptions to agentic, usage-based consumption is driving surging token demand, massive infrastructure investment, and adoption of token-efficiency tactics such as model routing and targeted post-training. Massive, high-quality upskilling prevents budget caps and known-ROI bias from stifling experimentation; discovery of high-value

videos.title: A Big Shift in the AI Race
videos.description:
Anthropic's forced shutdown of Mythos and Fable exposes jailbreak risks, cybersecurity weaknesses, and a chaotic regulatory response.  
SpaceX's IPO and the Cursor acquisition signal a shift toward monetizing massive compute and competing across models, harnesses, and control planes. Industry themes include token-cost pressures driving efficiency, KPMG evidence that top AI users treat models as reasoning partners, and escalating national-security oversight.
